output:
**(D) Approximately 2%**

This is a classic Bayes' theorem problem where base rate neglect trips people up.

**The math:**

| Group | Size (per 100,000) | Test result |
|---|---|---|
| Actually sick | 100 | 99 test positive (sensitivity 99%) |
| Healthy | 99,900 | 4,995 test positive (specificity 95% → 5% false positive) |

P(disease | positive) = 99 / (99 + 4,995) = 99 / 5,094 ≈ **1.94%**

**Why it's so low:** Even with an excellent test, when the disease is rare (1/1,000), the false positives from the huge healthy population vastly outnumber the true positives. ~98% of positive results are false alarms.
